title: Emergency contraception documentation

red_flags.text: Supports documenting intercourse timing, method discussion, pregnancy possibility and clinician-selected emergency-contraception care with method and eligibility qualifiers retained; it does not autonomously select or provide a method.
red_flags.rationale: Supports entered timing and clinician-selected emergency method only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting intercourse timing, method discussion, pregnancy possibility and clinician-selected emergency-contraception care with method and eligibility qualifiers retained; it does not autonomously select or provide a method.

source.title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition

exact_section.heading: Emergency contraceptive pills and copper-bearing intrauterine devices for emergency contraception

examination.text: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.
examination.evidence_extract: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.

exact_section.heading: 3.1 Examinations and tests; 3.2 Contraceptive eligibility; 5.1 Reasonable certainty that a woman is not pregnant

assessment.text: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Timing, cycle, pregnancy possibility, medicines, eligibility, consent, method availability and safeguarding context are absent.
No method, dose, device procedure, efficacy, pregnancy exclusion or legal conclusion is generated.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
